---
title: "Integrate across samples"
description: "Batch-correct across samples, then cluster and embed."
categories: [openpipeline]
order: 40
---

[`openpipeline`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline}<span class="swatch openpipeline"></span>

Integration is a **separate workflow** run after
[sample processing](process-samples.qmd). Samples from different donors, runs, or
preparations otherwise cluster by technical origin rather than biology; batch
correction realigns them, then clusters and embeds the result. Each method is its
own workflow running the same downstream chain.

## Options

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`integration/harmony_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/harmony_leiden.qmd) | Fast, interpretable default for most datasets |
| [`integration/scvi_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/scvi_leiden.qmd) | Many samples (>20), strong batch effects, large data |
| [`integration/scanorama_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/scanorama_leiden.qmd) | Batches with different cell-type compositions |
| [`integration/bbknn_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/bbknn_leiden.qmd) | Lightweight; graph only, no corrected embedding |
| [`integration/totalvi_leiden`](../reference/openpipeline/v4.2.0/workflows/integration/totalvi_leiden.qmd) | Matched RNA + ADT (CITE-seq); corrects both jointly |
| [`single_cell/parallel_integration`](../reference/openpipeline_composed/v0.3.0/single_cell/parallel_integration.qmd) | Run several methods on the same cohort and compare |

There is no universal best method; start with Harmony and switch if the batch
structure or modality demands it.

## Example: Harmony

For example, batch-correcting on `sample_id`:

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/integration/harmony_leiden/main.nf \
  --id cohort_01 \
  --input cohort_01_processed.h5mu \
  --obs_covariates "sample_id" \
  --leiden_resolution 1.0 \
  --output cohort_01_integrated.h5mu \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

**The method** (see the table): Harmony is the default; scVI for scale/complex
batches; totalVI for CITE-seq; Scanorama for uneven compositions; BBKNN when you
only need a graph.

**The covariate.** `--obs_covariates` (Harmony) / `--obs_batch` (scVI) must name
the `.obs` column identifying technical origin, usually sample, batch, or donor.

**Resolution.** `--leiden_resolution` controls cluster granularity: higher gives
more, smaller clusters.

**The downstream chain.** Every method then runs the same steps: batch correction
→ neighbor graph → Leiden clustering → UMAP. The corrected embedding, not the
UMAP, is what annotation and other downstream tools consume.

## Output

| Slot | Content |
|---|---|
| `.obsm["X_pca_integrated"]` | Batch-corrected embedding (name varies by method) |
| `.obs` Leiden labels | Cluster assignments |
| `.obsm` UMAP | 2-D coordinates for plotting |

Clusters are just numbers, assign biological labels next with
[cell type annotation](annotate.qmd).

:::{.callout-tip}
How the data's shape changes through these steps: see
[Advanced architecture](../concepts/data-state.qmd).
:::
